results.identified_issue: Interpreted 'remove that field entirely' as the user-selection UI (the composer's 'Answering as' section and Profile's account switcher), NOT the Answer.userId field. userId is kept deliberately: it's the mechanism that lets the Answers tab show only my answers and, later, distinguish other users' answers once a backend brings them in. UserStore still loads its [User] array from disk (older installs may hold several) so the active user's id survives — I just removed the add/switch/delete surface rather than migrating the persistence, which keeps existing answers matched to their author. If the intent was to also strip userId from the model, that's a follow-up.
results.summary: Collapsed the app to a single signed-in user and scoped the Answers tab to only my answers
results.detailed_description: You asked to simplify user setup down to the single person who's signed in. I made four changes. (1) Answers tab now shows only your answers — it filters by the current user's id, so seed answers and any future other-user answers don't appear there; the stats header and empty state follow the same filter. (2) The composer's 'Answering as' section is gone — since there's only you, every answer is obviously yours. (3) The Profile tab lost the 'Switch account' section entirely: no add/switch/delete of other accounts, just your avatar, name, answer count, Edit name, and Sign out. (4) UserStore lost its multi-user surface (add/setCurrent/delete); it keeps one signed-in user whose id is preserved across launches. I kept the underlying userId on each answer on purpose — it's what makes 'show only my answers' work and what will separate other people's answers from yours once a backend exists. Note: Discover still plays the full corpus at you (that's the point of the feed), so I only scoped the Answers tab. I verified by reading through every caller of the removed APIs (no stragglers) but did not build/run the app per the workflow.

description: Simplify the user setup. We don't need to be able to select between multiple users. There's only one user signed in per at a time. So any answer would obviously be from that one user. So you can remove that field entirely. And then on the account page, remove the option to select between different users. There's just the one user. There's just our user and then there's going to be other users that will leave other answers. so that in the answers page, the only answers that should be present are ones that come from the current user.
